content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ which contains pairwise distinct elements and a sequence B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​, which also contains pairwise distinct elements (but not necessarily distinct from elements of AAA). For each valid iii, 1≤Ai,Bi≤2⋅N1 \leq A_i, B_i \leq 2 \cdot N1≤Ai​,Bi​≤2⋅N.

You may rotate BBB as many times as you want. A rotation consists of moving the first element of the sequence to the end. Afterwards, let's define a sequence C1,C2,…,CNC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_NC1​,C2​,…,CN​ as Ci=(Ai+Bi)%NC_i = (A_i + B_i) \% NCi​=(Ai​+Bi​)%N for each valid iii.

There are NNN possible sequences CCC. Find the lexicographically smallest of these sequences.

**Note:** A sequence xxx is lexicographically smaller than a different sequence yyy if xi < yix_i < y_ixi​ < yi​, where iii is the smallest valid index where the sequences xxx and yyy differ.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers C1,C2,…,CNC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_NC1​,C2​,…,CN​ denoting the lexicographically smallest sequence.
constraints: 1≤Ai,Bi≤2⋅N1 \leq A_i, B_i \leq 2 \cdot N1≤Ai​,Bi​≤2⋅N for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: After rotating BBB once, it becomes (3,4,1)(3,4,1)(3,4,1). Now C=(1,2,0)C = (1,2,0)C=(1,2,0). This is the lexicographically smallest of all possible sequences CCC.
